course_meta.title: Sigma Web Development Course – Day-by-Day Roadmap

daily_schedule.practice_and_implement: 1 hour – Code along, implement examples, complete the practice task

roadmap.title: Setup, How Websites Work & Your First HTML Page

playlist_videos.title: Installing VS Code & How Websites Work
Basic Structure of an HTML Website

roadmap.watch_focus: Understand client-server model, install VS Code, write your very first HTML file with the correct boilerplate structure.
roadmap.practice_task: Create a file called index.html with the full HTML boilerplate (html, head, body tags). Add a heading saying 'Hello World' and a paragraph about yourself. Open it in the browser.

quiz.q: What does HTML stand for?
Which tag is the root element of every HTML page?
In the client-server model, which side runs in the user's browser?
What is the file extension for an HTML file?
Which VS Code extension helps you quickly scaffold HTML boilerplate?
What does the <head> section of an HTML document contain?
Which shortcut in VS Code triggers the Live Server extension to open your page?
What protocol do browsers use to request web pages from servers?
Which DOCTYPE declaration is used for HTML5?
What is the purpose of the <title> tag inside <head>?
options: Show a heading on the page

roadmap.title: Headings, Paragraphs, Links & Images

playlist_videos.title: Image, Lists and Tables in HTML

roadmap.watch_focus: Learn h1–h6 tags, paragraph tag, anchor tag with href, target attributes, img tag with src/alt, ordered and unordered lists, and basic HTML tables.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a mini 'About Me' page: use all heading levels, a paragraph, one external link, your photo (or placeholder image), a list of your hobbies, and a 3-column table showing your skills and proficiency.

quiz.q: Which HTML tag creates the largest heading?
What attribute of the <a> tag specifies the destination URL?
Which attribute makes a link open in a new browser tab?
Which tag is used to embed an image in HTML?
What does the 'alt' attribute on an tag do?
Which tag creates an unordered (bulleted) list?
In an HTML table, which tag defines a header cell?
Which tag wraps each row in an HTML table?
What does 'src' in the img tag stand for?
Which list type displays items with numbers by default?

roadmap.title: Forms, Inputs & Semantic HTML

playlist_videos.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
Forms and Input Tags in HTML
Inline and Block Elements in HTML

roadmap.watch_focus: Learn HTML forms (action, method), various input types (text, email, password, radio, checkbox, submit), labels, and understand inline vs block elements.
roadmap.practice_task: Create a Registration Form page with: Name (text), Email, Password, Gender (radio buttons), Accept Terms (checkbox), and a Submit button. Use proper <label> tags for accessibility.

quiz.q: Which attribute in a form specifies where the form data is sent?
What input type is used for password fields?
Which HTTP method sends form data in the request body (not the URL)?
What does 'for' attribute in a <label> tag do?
options: Links label to an input by matching its id
quiz.answer: Links label to an input by matching its id

quiz.q: Which input type allows selecting only one option from a group?
Which of the following is a BLOCK-level element?
Which of the following is an INLINE element?
What HTML5 semantic tag represents the main navigation links?
Which input attribute makes a field mandatory before form submission?
What does the <textarea> tag create?

roadmap.title: HTML5 Semantics, Media & Entities

playlist_videos.title: Video, Audio and Media in HTML

roadmap.watch_focus: Learn id and class attributes, embedding audio/video with HTML5 tags, and semantic elements like header, footer, article, section, aside.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a Blog Page layout using semantic HTML only: <header> with site name, <nav> with links, <main> with an <article>, an <aside> with related links, and a <footer>. Embed one YouTube video using <iframe>.

quiz.q: What is the difference between id and class attributes?
options: id is reusable, class is unique
id must be unique per page; class can be reused
quiz.answer: id must be unique per page; class can be reused

quiz.q: Which HTML5 tag is used to embed a video file natively?
Which attribute adds playback controls to <video> and <audio>?
Which semantic tag wraps the introductory content or navigation of a page?
What does the <article> tag semantically represent?
options: A self-contained piece of content like a blog post
quiz.answer: A self-contained piece of content like a blog post

quiz.q: Which tag is used to embed an external webpage or video (e.g., YouTube) inside your page?
What HTML entity is used to display a copyright symbol ©?
Which semantic tag is used for the bottom section of a page?
What is the purpose of using semantic HTML?
Which tag defines a section of content that is tangentially related to the main content?

roadmap.title: HTML Tables Deep Dive & Project – Pure HTML Media Player

playlist_videos.title: HTML Entities, Code Tag and More Tags

roadmap.watch_focus: Explore advanced HTML entities ( , <, >), <code>, <pre>, <abbr>, <blockquote> tags. Build the first full project: a media player using only HTML.
roadmap.practice_task: Complete Exercise 1 – build a Pure HTML Media Player. Use <audio> and <video> tags, add controls. Create a playlist of 3 songs/videos using anchor tags and proper semantic structure.

quiz.q: What does   represent in HTML?
Which tag is used to display inline code snippets?
What does the <pre> tag do?
Which HTML attribute loops a video continuously?
What does the <abbr> tag do?
options: Shows an abbreviation with a full form on hover via title attribute
quiz.answer: Shows an abbreviation with a full form on hover via title attribute

quiz.q: Which entity represents the less-than symbol < in HTML?
In a <table>, which element groups header rows?
The 'colspan' attribute in a table cell does what?
Which tag is used for longer quotations that are displayed as a block?
What is the correct way to add a source to the <audio> tag for browser compatibility?
options: Use multiple <source> tags inside <audio> with different formats
quiz.answer: Use multiple <source> tags inside <audio> with different formats

roadmap.title: Introduction to CSS – Selectors, Properties & Colors
roadmap.watch_focus: Understand what CSS is, three ways to add CSS (inline, internal, external), and key selectors: element, class, id, group, universal, attribute selectors.
roadmap.practice_task: Style your Day 2 'About Me' page using an external CSS file. Apply different selectors: style the h1 with element selector, add a class for highlighted text, use id for the main section background.

quiz.q: What does CSS stand for?
Which method of adding CSS has the highest specificity (inline)?
How do you select all elements with class 'highlight' in CSS?
Which selector selects ALL elements on the page?
What is the correct CSS property to change text color?
Which CSS value represents a fully transparent color?
What does the 'cascading' in CSS mean?
options: Styles fall from top to bottom, later rules overriding earlier ones
CSS files are loaded in parallel
quiz.answer: Styles fall from top to bottom, later rules overriding earlier ones

quiz.q: Which tag links an external CSS file in HTML?
How do you write a CSS comment?
Which CSS selector targets an element with id='main'?

roadmap.title: Box Model, Margin, Padding & Borders

playlist_videos.title: CSS Box Model – Margin, Padding and Borders
CSS Fonts, Text and Color Properties

roadmap.watch_focus: Master the CSS box model: content, padding, border, margin. Learn font-family, font-size, text-align, font-weight, CSS specificity hierarchy.
roadmap.practice_task: Create a styled 'Product Card' component: a box with padding, a border, margin, a product image, product title (custom font from Google Fonts), price, and a Buy button with hover styling.

quiz.q: What are the four areas of the CSS Box Model from inside to outside?
Which CSS property adds space INSIDE an element's border?
Which CSS property adds space OUTSIDE an element's border?
What does 'box-sizing: border-box' do?
options: Excludes padding and border from the total width
Includes padding and border in the total width
quiz.answer: Includes padding and border in the total width

quiz.q: Which property sets the font of an element?
Which CSS property aligns text horizontally?
In CSS specificity, which has the HIGHEST priority?
What value of 'margin' centers a block element horizontally?
Which property makes text bold?
CSS inheritance means child elements:
options: Automatically receive certain styles from parent elements
quiz.answer: Automatically receive certain styles from parent elements

roadmap.title: Display, Position & CSS Variables
roadmap.watch_focus: Learn display (block, inline, inline-block, none), CSS positioning (static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky), and CSS custom properties (variables) for theming.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a page with: a sticky header (position: sticky), a modal overlay (position: fixed), and a tooltip (position: relative + absolute child). Use CSS variables for your colour theme (--primary-color, --font-size-base).

quiz.q: Which display value hides an element completely from the layout?
What is the default position value of HTML elements?
Which position value removes an element from normal document flow and positions it relative to the nearest positioned ancestor?
Which position value keeps an element fixed relative to the viewport while scrolling?
What is the difference between 'visibility: hidden' and 'display: none'?
options: visibility: hidden hides the element but keeps its space; display: none removes it from layout
display: none keeps space; visibility removes it
Both remove the element from flow
quiz.answer: visibility: hidden hides the element but keeps its space; display: none removes it from layout

quiz.q: How do you declare a CSS variable named 'primary-color' in the :root?
How do you USE a CSS variable called '--font-size'?
Which position value makes an element scroll normally until it hits a defined offset, then sticks?
What does 'display: inline-block' do?
options: Element sits inline but respects width/height
quiz.answer: Element sits inline but respects width/height

quiz.q: The 'z-index' property only works on elements with which position values?

roadmap.title: Flexbox Layout
roadmap.watch_focus: Master Flexbox: flex-direction, justify-content, align-items, flex-wrap, flex-grow, flex-shrink, align-self. Build a functional responsive navbar.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a fully responsive navigation bar using Flexbox: logo on the left, nav links on the right, hamburger icon for mobile. Also create a 3-column feature section layout using Flexbox.

quiz.q: Which CSS property makes an element a flex container?
Which property defines the main axis direction in Flexbox?
What does 'justify-content: center' do in a flex container?
options: Centers items on the cross axis
Centers items on the main axis
quiz.answer: Centers items on the main axis

quiz.q: Which property aligns flex items on the CROSS axis?
What does 'flex-wrap: wrap' do?
options: Allows flex items to wrap to the next line if they overflow
quiz.answer: Allows flex items to wrap to the next line if they overflow

quiz.q: What does 'justify-content: space-between' do?
options: Puts equal space around all items
Places first item at start, last at end, equal space between
quiz.answer: Places first item at start, last at end, equal space between

quiz.q: Which property allows a single flex item to override its container's align-items?
What does 'flex: 1' shorthand mean?
Which value of flex-direction stacks items from bottom to top?
Which flexbox property sets the default size of a flex item before remaining space is distributed?

roadmap.title: CSS Grid Layout
roadmap.watch_focus: Learn CSS Grid: grid-template-columns, grid-template-rows, grid-gap, grid-area, fr unit, auto-fill and auto-fit for responsive grids.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a photo gallery using CSS Grid with auto-fill and minmax, so it becomes responsive without media queries. Also create a dashboard layout with a header, sidebar, main content, and footer using grid-area.

quiz.q: Which CSS property enables Grid layout on a container?
What does 'grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 1fr' create?
options: 3 columns where the middle is twice as wide
quiz.answer: 3 columns where the middle is twice as wide

quiz.q: What does the 'fr' unit stand for in CSS Grid?
Which function allows columns to be responsive without media queries?
What does 'grid-column: 1 / 3' mean?
options: Item spans from column line 1 to column line 3 (2 columns)
Item is in column 1 with 3 rows
quiz.answer: Item spans from column line 1 to column line 3 (2 columns)

quiz.q: Which property sets the gap between grid rows and columns?
What is 'grid-template-areas' used for?
options: Naming and placing grid areas visually in CSS
quiz.answer: Naming and placing grid areas visually in CSS

quiz.q: What is the difference between auto-fill and auto-fit in repeat()?
options: auto-fill creates empty tracks; auto-fit collapses them to fit content
auto-fit creates empty tracks; auto-fill collapses them
auto-fill is for rows, auto-fit for columns
quiz.answer: auto-fill creates empty tracks; auto-fit collapses them to fit content

quiz.q: Which property aligns grid items along the row (inline) axis within their cell?
Grid vs Flexbox – which statement is most accurate?
options: Flexbox is for 1D layouts (row OR column); Grid is for 2D layouts (rows AND columns)
Flexbox is better for complex page layouts
quiz.answer: Flexbox is for 1D layouts (row OR column); Grid is for 2D layouts (rows AND columns)

roadmap.title: Responsive Web Design & Media Queries
roadmap.watch_focus: Write mobile-first responsive designs with media queries. Learn CSS transforms (translate, rotate, scale, skew) and transitions/animations (@keyframes, animation properties).
roadmap.practice_task: Take your Grid photo gallery from Day 10 and make it fully responsive with media queries (show 1 col on mobile, 2 on tablet, 4 on desktop). Add a CSS bounce animation to an element on hover.

quiz.q: What is a CSS media query used for?
options: Applying styles based on device characteristics like screen width
quiz.answer: Applying styles based on device characteristics like screen width

quiz.q: Which breakpoint approach is recommended – mobile-first or desktop-first?
Which CSS property smoothly transitions a property change over time?
What does 'transform: translateX(50px)' do?
Which rule defines the steps in a CSS animation?
What does 'animation-iteration-count: infinite' do?
Which meta tag is required in HTML for responsive design to work on mobile devices?
What does 'transform: scale(2)' do to an element?
Which CSS property controls the speed curve of a transition or animation?
What does 'min-width: 768px' in a media query mean?
options: Apply styles on screens UP TO 768px wide
Apply styles on screens 768px wide AND ABOVE
quiz.answer: Apply styles on screens 768px wide AND ABOVE

roadmap.title: Pseudo-classes, Pseudo-elements & CSS Filters
roadmap.watch_focus: Learn :hover, :focus, :nth-child, :first-child, ::before, ::after pseudo elements. Apply box-shadow, text-shadow, CSS filter (blur, grayscale, brightness). Explore background properties.
roadmap.practice_task: Style a testimonials section: use ::before to add a decorative quote mark, :nth-child to alternate card background colours, box-shadow on cards, and apply a blur filter to an image on hover.

quiz.q: What is a CSS pseudo-class?
options: A CSS class added via JavaScript
A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element
A class defined in external libraries
quiz.answer: A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element

quiz.q: Which pseudo-class styles a link that the user's mouse is hovering over?
What does '::before' pseudo-element do?
options: Selects the element before in the DOM
Inserts content before the element's actual content
quiz.answer: Inserts content before the element's actual content

quiz.q: Which pseudo-class targets every EVEN child element?
What CSS property adds a shadow behind a box/element?
Which CSS property applies visual effects like blur or grayscale to an element?
What is the 'content' property required for when using ::before or ::after?
options: It defines what content to insert (can be empty string '')
quiz.answer: It defines what content to insert (can be empty string '')

quiz.q: Which pseudo-class targets the last child of its parent?
Which CSS property makes a background image non-repeating and cover the entire container?
What does 'filter: grayscale(100%)' do?
options: Makes the element completely black and white
quiz.answer: Makes the element completely black and white

roadmap.title: CSS Project – Full Website Clone / Portfolio
roadmap.watch_focus: Apply all CSS knowledge to build real-world styled components: profile card, multi-section coloured website, complete page layout.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a complete personal portfolio page (index.html + style.css): hero section, about section, skills section (CSS grid), projects section (card layout), and contact form. Must be responsive.

quiz.q: What CSS property creates a gradient background?
Which CSS property rounds the corners of an element?
How do you make a circle using only CSS on a div with equal width and height?
Which property controls how much an element is transparent?
Which CSS unit is relative to the font-size of the root element?
How do you add a Google Font to your CSS?
options: Both @import in CSS or <link> in HTML
quiz.answer: Both @import in CSS or <link> in HTML

quiz.q: Which property controls the space between letters?
What CSS technique is used to make images fill their container while maintaining aspect ratio?
Which property controls text decoration like underline and strikethrough?
What does 'overflow: hidden' do?
options: Clips content that overflows the element's box
quiz.answer: Clips content that overflows the element's box

roadmap.title: Introduction to JavaScript – Variables, Data Types & Operators

playlist_videos.title: JavaScript Variables, Data Types and Operators

roadmap.watch_focus: Learn what JavaScript is, where it runs, var/let/const, primitive data types (string, number, boolean, null, undefined), and operators (+, -, *, /, %, ===, !==, &&, ||).
roadmap.practice_task: Write a JS program in the browser console: declare variables for your name, age, and favourite language. Use conditionals to print 'You can vote' if age >= 18, otherwise 'Too young'. Use a switch for language to print a fun fact about it.

quiz.q: Which keyword declares a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned?
What is the result of 'typeof null' in JavaScript?
What does '===' check in JavaScript compared to '=='?
options: === checks value only; == checks value and type
=== checks value and type (strict equality); == checks value with type coercion
quiz.answer: === checks value and type (strict equality); == checks value with type coercion

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT a primitive data type in JavaScript?
What does 'let x = 5 + '3'' evaluate to in JavaScript?
What is the output of 'console.log(10 % 3)'?
Which operator is used for logical AND in JavaScript?
What value does an uninitialized variable have in JavaScript?
What is the correct way to write a single-line comment in JavaScript?
Which keyword is used to make a decision based on multiple specific values?

roadmap.title: Loops, Functions & Scope
roadmap.watch_focus: Master for, while, do-while loops. Understand function declarations vs expressions vs arrow functions. Learn scope (local, global, block), template literals, and string methods.
roadmap.practice_task: Write: (1) A loop that prints multiplication table of any number. (2) An arrow function that takes a name and age and returns a template literal greeting. (3) Use string methods to reverse a string, convert to uppercase, and find a substring.

quiz.q: What is the key difference between 'while' and 'do-while' loops?
options: do-while always executes at least once; while may not
while runs at least once; do-while may not
quiz.answer: do-while always executes at least once; while may not

quiz.q: What is an arrow function syntax for adding two numbers?
What does a template literal use to embed expressions?
What is the output of '"hello".toUpperCase()'?
Which loop is best when you know the exact number of iterations?
What does 'break' do inside a loop?
What is function hoisting?
options: Function declarations being moved to the top of their scope before execution
Calling a function with wrong arguments
quiz.answer: Function declarations being moved to the top of their scope before execution

quiz.q: What does 'str.indexOf('o')' return if 'o' is not found?
Which string method splits a string into an array?
What is variable scope?
options: Where a variable is accessible in the code
How much memory a variable uses
quiz.answer: Where a variable is accessible in the code

roadmap.title: Arrays & Array Methods
roadmap.watch_focus: Deep dive into arrays: creating, accessing, modifying. Key methods: push, pop, shift, unshift, splice, slice, map, filter, reduce, forEach, find. Introduction to JS Objects (key-value pairs).
roadmap.practice_task: Create an array of 10 students with marks. Use: filter() to get students who passed (>= 50), map() to scale marks to percentage, reduce() to find the average, and sort() to rank them. Display results using template literals.

quiz.q: Which method adds one or more elements to the END of an array?
What does 'array.map(fn)' return?
options: A new array with fn applied to each element
The original array modified in place
quiz.answer: A new array with fn applied to each element

quiz.q: What does 'array.filter(fn)' return?
options: A new array with elements where fn returns true
quiz.answer: A new array with elements where fn returns true

quiz.q: What does 'array.reduce((acc, curr) => acc + curr, 0)' compute?
Which method removes the FIRST element of an array and returns it?
What is the difference between 'slice' and 'splice'?
options: slice returns a portion without modifying original; splice modifies the original array
splice doesn't modify original; slice does
quiz.answer: slice returns a portion without modifying original; splice modifies the original array

quiz.q: How do you access the value of key 'name' in object 'person'?
Which array method finds the FIRST element satisfying a condition?
What does 'array.includes(value)' return?
options: A boolean: true if value exists, false otherwise
quiz.answer: A boolean: true if value exists, false otherwise

quiz.q: How do you create an object in JavaScript?

roadmap.title: DOM Manipulation
roadmap.watch_focus: Understand the DOM tree. Select elements using getElementById, querySelector, querySelectorAll. Change innerHTML, textContent, styles, and classes. Handle click, input, keydown, and submit events.
roadmap.practice_task: Build an interactive To-Do List app: input field + add button adds tasks to a list dynamically using DOM manipulation. Each task has a 'Delete' button. Use event listeners throughout. No frameworks.

quiz.q: What does DOM stand for?
Which method selects the FIRST element matching a CSS selector?
What is the difference between 'innerHTML' and 'textContent'?
options: innerHTML parses HTML; textContent treats content as plain text
quiz.answer: innerHTML parses HTML; textContent treats content as plain text

quiz.q: Which method is used to add an event listener to an element?
How do you add a CSS class to an element using JavaScript?
What does 'document.createElement('div')' do?
options: Creates a new div element in memory (not yet in DOM)
quiz.answer: Creates a new div element in memory (not yet in DOM)

quiz.q: Which method adds a child element to the end of a parent element?
How do you prevent the default browser behavior in an event (like form submission)?
What does 'querySelectorAll' return?
options: A NodeList of all matching elements
quiz.answer: A NodeList of all matching elements

quiz.q: How do you change the inline style of an element's font size to 20px?

roadmap.title: Advanced JavaScript – Callbacks, Promises & Async/Await

playlist_videos.title: JavaScript Callbacks and Higher Order Functions

roadmap.watch_focus: Understand asynchronous JavaScript: the event loop, callbacks and callback hell, Promises (.then, .catch, .finally), and cleaner async/await syntax with try/catch error handling.
roadmap.practice_task: Fetch data from a free public API (e.g., https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts) using both: (1) Promises with .then().catch(), and (2) async/await. Display the first 5 post titles on the page.

quiz.q: What is a callback function?
options: A function passed as an argument to another function and called later
A function that returns a promise
quiz.answer: A function passed as an argument to another function and called later

quiz.q: What is 'callback hell'?
options: Callbacks running in the wrong order
Deeply nested callbacks that become hard to read and maintain
Too many callbacks in one function
quiz.answer: Deeply nested callbacks that become hard to read and maintain

quiz.q: What are the three states of a JavaScript Promise?
Which Promise method runs regardless of whether the promise was fulfilled or rejected?
What does 'await' do inside an async function?
options: Waits for a Promise to settle before continuing to the next line
quiz.answer: Waits for a Promise to settle before continuing to the next line

quiz.q: How do you handle errors with async/await?
options: Errors cannot occur in async functions

quiz.q: Which built-in function is used to make HTTP requests using Promises in modern JS?
What must a function be declared as to use 'await' inside it?
What does 'Promise.all([p1, p2, p3])' do?
options: Resolves when ALL promises resolve; rejects if ANY one rejects
Resolves when the first promise resolves
quiz.answer: Resolves when ALL promises resolve; rejects if ANY one rejects

quiz.q: Is JavaScript single-threaded or multi-threaded?
options: Single-threaded with an asynchronous event loop
quiz.answer: Single-threaded with an asynchronous event loop

roadmap.title: Fetch API, JSON & Working with REST APIs

playlist_videos.title: Fetch API and JSON in JavaScript
JavaScript Error Handling – Try, Catch, Finally

roadmap.watch_focus: Master the Fetch API for GET/POST requests, JSON.parse() and JSON.stringify(), error handling with try/catch, and introduction to OOP with classes, constructors, inheritance.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a Weather App: use the OpenWeatherMap free API (or JSONPlaceholder). Create a class 'WeatherCard' with a constructor. Fetch data with async/await, handle errors gracefully, and render data to DOM.

quiz.q: What does 'JSON.parse()' do?
options: Converts JavaScript object to a JSON string
Converts a JSON string to a JavaScript object
quiz.answer: Converts a JSON string to a JavaScript object

quiz.q: What does 'JSON.stringify()' do?
options: Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string
quiz.answer: Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string

quiz.q: What is the correct way to read JSON from a fetch response?
In OOP, what is a 'constructor' in a JS class?
options: A method called when the class is destroyed
A special method called when a new object is created with 'new'
quiz.answer: A special method called when a new object is created with 'new'

quiz.q: What keyword is used to create an instance of a class?
What does 'extends' do in a JavaScript class?
options: Adds new methods to built-in types
Creates a child class that inherits from a parent class
quiz.answer: Creates a child class that inherits from a parent class

quiz.q: What does 'super()' do inside a child class constructor?
options: Creates a copy of the parent

quiz.q: What HTTP method is typically used to send data to create a new resource on a server?
Which HTTP status code indicates a successful GET request?
In the fetch API, how do you make a POST request with JSON body?

roadmap.title: LocalStorage, SessionStorage & JavaScript Modules
roadmap.watch_focus: Learn browser storage (localStorage, sessionStorage), ES6 modules (import/export, default vs named exports), and closures/scope chain.
roadmap.practice_task: Upgrade your To-Do app from Day 17 to persist tasks using localStorage (save on add, load on page refresh, delete persisted items). Refactor into modules: one module for storage, one for UI rendering.

quiz.q: What is the key difference between localStorage and sessionStorage?
options: localStorage persists until manually cleared; sessionStorage clears when the browser tab is closed
sessionStorage is permanent; localStorage is temporary
quiz.answer: localStorage persists until manually cleared; sessionStorage clears when the browser tab is closed

quiz.q: How do you save a string in localStorage?
How do you retrieve a value from localStorage?
localStorage can only store which type of data?
options: Strings (objects must be serialized with JSON.stringify)
quiz.answer: Strings (objects must be serialized with JSON.stringify)

quiz.q: What keyword exports a function/variable from a JS module?
What is the difference between 'default export' and 'named export'?
options: A module can have only one default export but multiple named exports
Default exports use curly braces when importing
quiz.answer: A module can have only one default export but multiple named exports

quiz.q: How do you import a named export 'add' from 'math.js'?
What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function that closes when called
A function that retains access to its outer scope's variables even after the outer function returns
quiz.answer: A function that retains access to its outer scope's variables even after the outer function returns

quiz.q: What does 'localStorage.removeItem('key')' do?
options: Throws an error if key doesn't exist

quiz.q: Which script attribute is required to use ES6 import/export in the browser?

roadmap.title: Git & GitHub – Version Control
roadmap.watch_focus: Learn Git (init, add, commit, status, log), GitHub (push, pull, clone), branching (branch, checkout, merge), merge conflicts, and deploy a static site to GitHub Pages.
roadmap.practice_task: Initialize a Git repo for your portfolio project. Create a 'dev' branch, make changes (add a new section), merge to 'main', push to GitHub, and deploy via GitHub Pages. Your portfolio should be live on the internet.

quiz.q: What does 'git init' do?
options: Initializes a new local Git repository
quiz.answer: Initializes a new local Git repository

quiz.q: What does 'git add .' do?
options: Stages all changed files for the next commit
quiz.answer: Stages all changed files for the next commit

quiz.q: What is the correct command to commit staged changes with a message?
Which command creates and immediately switches to a new branch?
What does 'git merge feature' do when you're on the main branch?
options: Merges the feature branch into main
quiz.answer: Merges the feature branch into main

quiz.q: What is a merge conflict?
options: When two commits have the same message
When two branches have changes to the same lines that Git can't auto-resolve
When a branch is deleted before merging
quiz.answer: When two branches have changes to the same lines that Git can't auto-resolve

quiz.q: Which command links your local repo to a GitHub remote repository?
What does 'git pull' do?
options: Fetches and merges changes from the remote into your local branch
quiz.answer: Fetches and merges changes from the remote into your local branch

quiz.q: Where are GitHub Pages sites hosted from?
options: Directly from a GitHub repository's main or gh-pages branch
quiz.answer: Directly from a GitHub repository's main or gh-pages branch

quiz.q: What does '.gitignore' file do?
options: Specifies files and folders Git should NOT track
quiz.answer: Specifies files and folders Git should NOT track

roadmap.title: Advanced JS – Destructuring, Spread/Rest & ES6+ Features
roadmap.watch_focus: ES6+ features: array/object destructuring, spread operator (...), rest parameters, Map and Set data structures.
roadmap.practice_task: Refactor your weather or to-do app using ES6 features: use destructuring when reading API responses, spread to merge objects, rest parameters in a function that accepts variable arguments. Replace suitable arrays with Sets to ensure uniqueness.

quiz.q: What does array destructuring do? Example: const [a, b] = [1, 2]
options: Unpacks values from an array into individual variables
quiz.answer: Unpacks values from an array into individual variables

quiz.q: What does object destructuring do? Example: const {name, age} = person
options: Creates a copy of the object
Converts the object to an array

quiz.q: What does the spread operator (...) do when used with an array?
What is the purpose of rest parameters in a function?
options: Collects all remaining arguments into an array
quiz.answer: Collects all remaining arguments into an array

quiz.q: What is a JavaScript Set?
options: An object similar to an array but with only unique values
A method to set variable values
quiz.answer: An object similar to an array but with only unique values

quiz.q: How is a JavaScript Map different from a plain object?
options: Map keys must be strings; objects can use any type
Map can use any value as key (not just strings), preserves insertion order, and has size property
Objects have more methods than Maps
quiz.answer: Map can use any value as key (not just strings), preserves insertion order, and has size property

quiz.q: How do you merge two objects using the spread operator?
What does 'const {a: newName} = obj' do in destructuring?
options: Renames the property a to newName in the original object
Extracts property a and assigns it to a variable called newName
Creates a copy with property renamed
quiz.answer: Extracts property a and assigns it to a variable called newName

quiz.q: How do you add an element to a Set?
What happens if you add a duplicate value to a Set?
options: It is silently ignored; Set only keeps unique values
quiz.answer: It is silently ignored; Set only keeps unique values

roadmap.title: Introduction to Node.js
roadmap.watch_focus: What is Node.js and why use it on the server. CommonJS modules (require/module.exports). Built-in modules: fs (read/write files), path, os. npm: install packages, package.json, node_modules.
roadmap.practice_task: Create a Node.js script that: reads a text file, counts the number of words, writes a result summary to a new file. Also install the 'chalk' npm package and use it to print colorful console messages.

quiz.q: What is Node.js?
options: A JavaScript runtime environment built on Chrome's V8 engine for server-side use
quiz.answer: A JavaScript runtime environment built on Chrome's V8 engine for server-side use

quiz.q: What does 'require()' do in Node.js?
options: Loads/imports a module (built-in, local, or npm)
Requires a variable to be defined
quiz.answer: Loads/imports a module (built-in, local, or npm)

quiz.q: Which Node.js built-in module handles file system operations?
What is 'package.json' in an npm project?
options: A configuration file listing project metadata and dependencies
A JavaScript file for package logic
The main entry point of the app
A file that stores environment variables
quiz.answer: A configuration file listing project metadata and dependencies

quiz.q: Which command initializes a new npm project and creates package.json?
What does 'npm install express' do?
options: Installs the Express package and adds it to dependencies in package.json
quiz.answer: Installs the Express package and adds it to dependencies in package.json

quiz.q: What should be included in .gitignore for Node.js projects?
What is the difference between 'fs.readFileSync' and 'fs.readFile'?
options: readFileSync is asynchronous; readFile is synchronous
readFileSync is synchronous (blocks execution); readFile is asynchronous (callback-based)
quiz.answer: readFileSync is synchronous (blocks execution); readFile is asynchronous (callback-based)

quiz.q: What does 'module.exports = myFunction' do in Node.js?
options: Makes myFunction available to other files that require this module
quiz.answer: Makes myFunction available to other files that require this module

quiz.q: Which command runs a Node.js file called 'app.js'?

roadmap.title: Express.js – Building a Web Server

playlist_videos.title: Express – Serving Static Files and EJS Templates

roadmap.watch_focus: Build HTTP servers with Express: app.get, app.post, routing, middleware (express.json, express.static), and server-side templating with EJS.
roadmap.practice_task: Build an Express server with: a home route (GET /), an about route, a POST /contact route that logs form data. Serve a static HTML/CSS folder. Use EJS to render a dynamic page showing current date and a list of items.

quiz.q: What is Express.js?
options: A minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework
quiz.answer: A minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework

quiz.q: Which Express method handles GET requests?
What are Express middleware functions?
options: Functions that run between the database and client
Functions that have access to req, res, and next, running in the request-response cycle
quiz.answer: Functions that have access to req, res, and next, running in the request-response cycle

quiz.q: Which middleware is used to parse incoming JSON request bodies in Express?
What does 'res.send()' do in Express?
options: Sends an HTTP response to the client
quiz.answer: Sends an HTTP response to the client

quiz.q: How do you serve static files (like HTML, CSS, images) in Express?
What is EJS in Express?
options: A template engine that allows embedding JavaScript in HTML files
quiz.answer: A template engine that allows embedding JavaScript in HTML files

quiz.q: What does 'next()' do inside an Express middleware?
options: Passes control to the next matching middleware or route handler
Goes to the next line of code
quiz.answer: Passes control to the next matching middleware or route handler

quiz.q: How do you define a route parameter in Express (e.g., for user ID)?
What port does Express listen on by default in tutorials, and how do you start listening?

roadmap.title: MongoDB & Mongoose

playlist_videos.title: Mongoose Schema, Model and CRUD Operations

roadmap.watch_focus: Learn MongoDB (NoSQL, documents, collections), connect to MongoDB with Mongoose, define Schemas and Models, perform CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete) with Mongoose methods.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a simple Blog API: create a Mongoose 'Post' model (title, content, author, date). Write Express routes for: GET all posts, GET post by ID, POST new post, PUT update post, DELETE post. Test with Thunder Client or Postman.

quiz.q: What type of database is MongoDB?
What is a MongoDB document?
options: A PDF file stored in the database
A record stored as a JSON-like BSON object
quiz.answer: A record stored as a JSON-like BSON object

quiz.q: What is Mongoose?
options: An ODM (Object Data Modeling) library that provides a schema-based solution to model MongoDB data in Node.js
quiz.answer: An ODM (Object Data Modeling) library that provides a schema-based solution to model MongoDB data in Node.js

quiz.q: What does a Mongoose Schema define?
options: The structure, types, and validation rules of a document
quiz.answer: The structure, types, and validation rules of a document

quiz.q: Which Mongoose method saves a new document to the database?
Which Mongoose method retrieves ALL documents from a collection?
Which Mongoose method finds a document by its '_id' field?
Which Mongoose method updates a document and returns the updated version?
What is the difference between MongoDB and SQL databases?
options: MongoDB uses tables; SQL uses documents
MongoDB stores data in flexible JSON-like documents; SQL uses fixed-schema tables with relations
SQL is faster in all cases
quiz.answer: MongoDB stores data in flexible JSON-like documents; SQL uses fixed-schema tables with relations

quiz.q: What connection string is used to connect Mongoose to a local MongoDB instance?

roadmap.title: REST API – Authentication with Sessions & Cookies
roadmap.watch_focus: Implement user authentication: sessions (express-session), cookies, password hashing with bcrypt, signup and login routes, protecting routes, and flash messages.
roadmap.practice_task: Add authentication to your Blog API: create User model (username, email, hashedPassword). Build /signup and /login routes (hash passwords with bcrypt). Protect the POST/PUT/DELETE routes so only logged-in users can use them. Add flash messages for feedback.

quiz.q: What is password hashing?
options: Encrypting a password so it can be decrypted later
A one-way transformation of a password into a fixed-length string that cannot be reversed
quiz.answer: A one-way transformation of a password into a fixed-length string that cannot be reversed

quiz.q: Which npm package is commonly used for password hashing in Node.js?
What does 'bcrypt.compare(password, hash)' return?
options: A boolean-resolved Promise: true if password matches hash
quiz.answer: A boolean-resolved Promise: true if password matches hash

quiz.q: What is an HTTP session?
options: A permanent connection between client and server
Server-side storage of user data across multiple requests, linked via a session ID stored in a cookie
quiz.answer: Server-side storage of user data across multiple requests, linked via a session ID stored in a cookie

quiz.q: What is a cookie in web development?
options: A small piece of data stored in the browser, sent with HTTP requests to the server
quiz.answer: A small piece of data stored in the browser, sent with HTTP requests to the server

quiz.q: What 'salt' rounds in bcrypt affect?
options: The output length of the hash
The number of hashing iterations – more rounds = more secure but slower
quiz.answer: The number of hashing iterations – more rounds = more secure but slower

quiz.q: How do you protect a route so only authenticated users can access it?
options: Create a middleware that checks req.session.user and redirects to login if not authenticated
quiz.answer: Create a middleware that checks req.session.user and redirects to login if not authenticated

quiz.q: What are flash messages in Express?
options: One-time messages stored in session (using connect-flash) displayed once then cleared
quiz.answer: One-time messages stored in session (using connect-flash) displayed once then cleared

quiz.q: What does 'req.session.destroy()' do?
options: Deletes all sessions in the database
Destroys the current user's session (used for logout)
Clears only the session ID cookie
quiz.answer: Destroys the current user's session (used for logout)

quiz.q: Why should you never store plain text passwords in a database?
options: Plain text takes more storage space
If the database is breached, all user passwords are immediately exposed
quiz.answer: If the database is breached, all user passwords are immediately exposed

roadmap.title: Full-Stack Mini Project – Express + MongoDB + EJS

playlist_videos.title: Building a Full-Stack App with Express, MongoDB, and EJS

roadmap.watch_focus: Apply MVC pattern (Models, Views, Controllers) to structure an Express app. Build a complete CRUD application (task manager or blog) with MongoDB. Deploy to a platform like Railway or Render.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a full-stack Task Manager: MVC structured, MongoDB backend, EJS views, user authentication, full CRUD for tasks. Deploy to Render.com or Railway. Share the live URL.

quiz.q: What does MVC stand for?
In MVC, what is the role of the Model?
options: Represents the data layer and business logic (database interaction)
quiz.answer: Represents the data layer and business logic (database interaction)

quiz.q: In MVC, what is the role of the View?
options: Renders the UI/templates that the user sees
quiz.answer: Renders the UI/templates that the user sees

quiz.q: In MVC, what is the role of the Controller?
options: Processes incoming requests, interacts with Model, and returns the appropriate View or response
quiz.answer: Processes incoming requests, interacts with Model, and returns the appropriate View or response

quiz.q: What environment variable file is commonly used to store secrets in Node.js?
Which npm package reads .env files in Node.js?
What does 'process.env.PORT' refer to?
options: The port number from the system environment variables (used in deployment)
quiz.answer: The port number from the system environment variables (used in deployment)

quiz.q: Why is structuring an app with MVC beneficial?
options: Improves code organization, maintainability, and separation of concerns
quiz.answer: Improves code organization, maintainability, and separation of concerns

quiz.q: What is a Procfile used for in deployment (e.g., Heroku/Render)?
options: Declaring the command used to start the app on the server
quiz.answer: Declaring the command used to start the app on the server

quiz.q: What is a cloud MongoDB database service commonly used in deployment?

roadmap.title: Introduction to React – JSX, Components & Props
roadmap.watch_focus: What is React, why use it, Create React App / Vite setup, JSX syntax, functional components, passing data via props, prop types.
roadmap.practice_task: Set up a React project with Vite. Build a reusable 'ProductCard' component that accepts props: name, price, image, description. Render 5 different products by passing different props. Style with CSS modules.

quiz.q: What is React?
options: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Meta
quiz.answer: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Meta

quiz.q: What is JSX?
options: A syntax extension for JavaScript that looks like HTML and is used to describe UI in React
quiz.answer: A syntax extension for JavaScript that looks like HTML and is used to describe UI in React

quiz.q: In JSX, how do you use 'class' for CSS?
What are props in React?
options: Properties passed from parent to child components (read-only data)
quiz.answer: Properties passed from parent to child components (read-only data)

quiz.q: Can a child component modify the props it receives?
options: No, props are read-only in the child
Only if the prop is an object
quiz.answer: No, props are read-only in the child

quiz.q: What is the entry point component in a typical React app?
How do you embed a JavaScript expression inside JSX?
What does 'React.Fragment' or '<>' </> solve?
options: Allows returning multiple elements without adding an extra DOM node
It is a type of hook
quiz.answer: Allows returning multiple elements without adding an extra DOM node

quiz.q: What tool is commonly used to quickly set up a React project?
How do you render a list of items in React (e.g., an array of names)?
options: Use a for loop directly in JSX
Use array.map() to return JSX for each item, with a unique 'key' prop
quiz.answer: Use array.map() to return JSX for each item, with a unique 'key' prop

roadmap.title: React State & Hooks – useState & useEffect
roadmap.watch_focus: Master useState for component state, useEffect for side effects (fetching data, subscriptions, timers), dependency array, cleanup functions. Conditional rendering and rendering lists.
roadmap.practice_task: Build a 'GitHub User Search' React app: an input field updates state with each keystroke (useState), on button click fetch GitHub API (useEffect or async handler), conditionally show loading/error/results states, render repos as a list.

quiz.q: What is React state?
options: Data that belongs to a component and changes over time, causing re-renders
quiz.answer: Data that belongs to a component and changes over time, causing re-renders

quiz.q: What does useState return?
options: An object with get and set

quiz.q: What happens when you call the state setter function?
options: The state updates and React re-renders the component
Only the variable value changes without re-render
quiz.answer: The state updates and React re-renders the component

quiz.q: What is the purpose of useEffect?
options: To perform side effects (data fetching, subscriptions, DOM manipulation) after render
quiz.answer: To perform side effects (data fetching, subscriptions, DOM manipulation) after render

quiz.q: What does an empty dependency array '[]' in useEffect mean?
options: Effect runs only once after the initial mount
quiz.answer: Effect runs only once after the initial mount

quiz.q: What does the cleanup function returned from useEffect do?
options: Runs before the next effect or when component unmounts, used to clean up subscriptions/timers
quiz.answer: Runs before the next effect or when component unmounts, used to clean up subscriptions/timers

quiz.q: What is conditional rendering in React?
options: Rendering different JSX based on a condition using &&, ternary, or if/else
quiz.answer: Rendering different JSX based on a condition using &&, ternary, or if/else

quiz.q: What is the 'key' prop required for when rendering lists?
options: Helps React identify which items changed, were added, or removed for efficient reconciliation
It is the array index always
quiz.answer: Helps React identify which items changed, were added, or removed for efficient reconciliation

quiz.q: When should you avoid using array index as the 'key' prop?
options: When the list can be reordered or items can be deleted, as it causes bugs
When list has more than 10 items
Index is never allowed as key
quiz.answer: When the list can be reordered or items can be deleted, as it causes bugs

quiz.q: Which hook should you use to fetch data when a component first mounts?

roadmap.title: React Router & Forms
roadmap.watch_focus: Client-side routing with React Router v6: Routes, Route, Link, NavLink, useNavigate, useParams. Controlled form components with onChange handlers. useRef for DOM access. useContext for global state.
roadmap.practice_task: Extend your portfolio into a multi-page React SPA: Home, About, Projects, Contact pages using React Router. Add a controlled form on the Contact page that validates input before 'submitting'. Use useContext to share theme (dark/light) across all pages.

quiz.q: What is React Router used for?
options: Client-side routing in React SPAs (Single Page Applications)
quiz.answer: Client-side routing in React SPAs (Single Page Applications)

quiz.q: Which React Router component wraps your entire routing setup?
How do you navigate programmatically in React Router v6?
What is a controlled component in React forms?
options: A component controlled by the parent
A form element whose value is controlled by React state (not the DOM)
quiz.answer: A form element whose value is controlled by React state (not the DOM)

quiz.q: How do you access URL parameters in React Router v6?
What is useRef primarily used for?
options: Persisting mutable values across renders and accessing DOM elements without causing re-renders
quiz.answer: Persisting mutable values across renders and accessing DOM elements without causing re-renders

quiz.q: What problem does useContext solve?
options: Prop drilling – passing props through many levels of components by providing a global store
quiz.answer: Prop drilling – passing props through many levels of components by providing a global store

quiz.q: What is prop drilling?
options: Using props to drill into DOM
Passing props through multiple component layers that don't need them, just to reach a deeply nested component
quiz.answer: Passing props through multiple component layers that don't need them, just to reach a deeply nested component

quiz.q: What is a SPA (Single Page Application)?
options: A website with one HTML page that dynamically updates content without full page reloads
An app with a single CSS file
quiz.answer: A website with one HTML page that dynamically updates content without full page reloads

quiz.q: What is the difference between <Link> and <NavLink> in React Router?
options: NavLink adds an 'active' CSS class automatically when the link matches the current route
quiz.answer: NavLink adds an 'active' CSS class automatically when the link matches the current route

roadmap.title: Advanced Hooks – useReducer, useMemo & Custom Hooks
roadmap.watch_focus: useReducer for complex state logic, useMemo for expensive calculations memoization, useCallback for function memoization, building reusable custom hooks (useFetch, useLocalStorage).
roadmap.practice_task: Refactor your To-Do app using useReducer for all actions (ADD, DELETE, TOGGLE, CLEAR). Create a custom useFetch hook that handles loading/error/data states. Use useMemo to compute statistics (total, completed, pending).

quiz.q: When should you prefer useReducer over useState?
options: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or next state depends on previous
When there are no side effects
quiz.answer: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or next state depends on previous

quiz.q: What does a reducer function take as arguments?
What does useReducer return?
What does 'useMemo' do?
options: Memoizes the result of an expensive calculation, recomputing only when dependencies change
quiz.answer: Memoizes the result of an expensive calculation, recomputing only when dependencies change

quiz.q: What is the difference between useMemo and useCallback?
options: useMemo memoizes a computed value; useCallback memoizes a function reference
useCallback is for values; useMemo is for functions
quiz.answer: useMemo memoizes a computed value; useCallback memoizes a function reference

quiz.q: What is a custom hook?
options: A JavaScript function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks and encapsulates reusable stateful logic
quiz.answer: A JavaScript function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks and encapsulates reusable stateful logic

quiz.q: Why must custom hook names start with 'use'?
options: It's just a convention with no enforcement
React's linter enforces this to apply rules of hooks to custom hooks
quiz.answer: React's linter enforces this to apply rules of hooks to custom hooks

quiz.q: What is 'dispatch' in useReducer?
options: A function to send actions to the reducer to update state
quiz.answer: A function to send actions to the reducer to update state

quiz.q: When would you NOT need useMemo?
options: For all simple computations where the performance gain doesn't justify the complexity
For derived data depending on large arrays
quiz.answer: For all simple computations where the performance gain doesn't justify the complexity

quiz.q: What is an 'action' in the context of useReducer?
options: A plain object (usually with 'type' and optional 'payload') dispatched to the reducer describing what happened
quiz.answer: A plain object (usually with 'type' and optional 'payload') dispatched to the reducer describing what happened

roadmap.title: React + API Integration & Deployment

playlist_videos.title: Fetching Data in React with Axios

roadmap.watch_focus: Use Axios for API calls (vs fetch), connect React frontend to your Express backend API (CORS), deploy React app to Vercel or Netlify.
roadmap.practice_task: Connect your React portfolio/app to your Express + MongoDB backend. Display blog posts fetched from your own API. Handle CORS on the backend. Deploy React to Vercel and backend to Render. Test the full-stack app live.

quiz.q: What is Axios?
options: A promise-based HTTP client for browser and Node.js
quiz.answer: A promise-based HTTP client for browser and Node.js

quiz.q: What is CORS?
options: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing – a mechanism allowing or blocking web requests from different domains
quiz.answer: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing – a mechanism allowing or blocking web requests from different domains

quiz.q: Why does a React app (on localhost:3000) get a CORS error when calling an Express API (on localhost:5000)?
options: They are on different origins (different ports), so the browser blocks the request by default
quiz.answer: They are on different origins (different ports), so the browser blocks the request by default

quiz.q: Which npm package enables CORS in an Express app?
What is an advantage of Axios over fetch?
options: Axios is built into browsers; fetch is not
Axios automatically transforms JSON responses, supports request cancellation, and has better error handling
Axios is faster in all cases
Fetch is only for GET requests
quiz.answer: Axios automatically transforms JSON responses, supports request cancellation, and has better error handling

quiz.q: What platform is commonly used to deploy React frontend apps for free?
What file do you create in React to set API base URLs for development vs production?
What does Vite use instead of webpack for development bundling?
options: Rollup (for production) and native ESM with esbuild (for dev)
quiz.answer: Rollup (for production) and native ESM with esbuild (for dev)

quiz.q: What command builds a React app for production?
What is the 'proxy' field in package.json used for in CRA projects?
options: Redirecting API calls in development to avoid CORS issues
quiz.answer: Redirecting API calls in development to avoid CORS issues

roadmap.title: React Project – Full E-Commerce or Blog App
roadmap.watch_focus: Build a complete React project integrating all concepts: component hierarchy, state management, API integration, routing, forms, and deployment.
roadmap.practice_task: Complete and polish the blog app project from tutorials. Must have: user-friendly UI, all CRUD features working, protected routes (if auth implemented), error boundaries, loading states, and be deployed with a shareable URL.

quiz.q: What is 'lifting state up' in React?
options: Moving state to a CSS file
Moving state to a higher parent component so it can be shared between siblings
quiz.answer: Moving state to a higher parent component so it can be shared between siblings

quiz.q: What is component composition in React?
options: Building complex UIs by combining smaller, reusable components
quiz.answer: Building complex UIs by combining smaller, reusable components

quiz.q: What is an Error Boundary in React?
options: A class component that catches JavaScript errors in its child component tree and displays a fallback UI
quiz.answer: A class component that catches JavaScript errors in its child component tree and displays a fallback UI

quiz.q: What is React.lazy used for?
options: Code-splitting: lazily loading components only when they are needed
quiz.answer: Code-splitting: lazily loading components only when they are needed

quiz.q: What does 'Suspense' component do in React?
options: Suspends the app until data loads
Displays a fallback UI (like a loading spinner) while a lazy-loaded component is being fetched
quiz.answer: Displays a fallback UI (like a loading spinner) while a lazy-loaded component is being fetched

quiz.q: What is the Virtual DOM in React?
options: An in-memory representation of the real DOM; React uses it to efficiently compute minimal updates
quiz.answer: An in-memory representation of the real DOM; React uses it to efficiently compute minimal updates

quiz.q: What is reconciliation in React?
options: The process React uses to compare old and new Virtual DOMs and update only changed parts of the real DOM
quiz.answer: The process React uses to compare old and new Virtual DOMs and update only changed parts of the real DOM

quiz.q: What is the purpose of the 'key' prop in a list – why must it be unique and stable?
options: Keys must be unique to avoid duplicate rendering and stable to prevent React unmounting/remounting components unnecessarily
Keys are used for CSS targeting
Keys are only for performance logging
quiz.answer: Keys must be unique to avoid duplicate rendering and stable to prevent React unmounting/remounting components unnecessarily

quiz.q: What is state management in the context of large React apps?
options: Organizing and handling shared application state (across many components) using tools like Context API, Redux, or Zustand
quiz.answer: Organizing and handling shared application state (across many components) using tools like Context API, Redux, or Zustand

quiz.q: What makes React a 'declarative' library?
options: You describe what the UI should look like for a given state; React handles how to update the DOM to match
quiz.answer: You describe what the UI should look like for a given state; React handles how to update the DOM to match

roadmap.title: Introduction to Next.js – Pages, Routing & SSR

playlist_videos.title: Next.js Server-Side Rendering (SSR) and Static Site Generation (SSG)

roadmap.watch_focus: What is Next.js and why use it over plain React. App Router vs Pages Router, file-based routing, Link component, SSR (getServerSideProps) vs SSG (getStaticProps), incremental static regeneration (ISR).
roadmap.practice_task: Initialize a Next.js project. Create 4 pages (Home, Blog, Blog/[id], About) using file-based routing. Fetch blog posts statically (getStaticProps) from JSONPlaceholder. Individual post pages use getStaticPaths + getStaticProps.

quiz.q: What is Next.js?
options: A React framework that adds SSR, SSG, file-based routing, and API routes, built on top of React
quiz.answer: A React framework that adds SSR, SSG, file-based routing, and API routes, built on top of React

quiz.q: What is Server-Side Rendering (SSR)?
options: HTML generated on the server for each request and sent to the client
quiz.answer: HTML generated on the server for each request and sent to the client

quiz.q: What is Static Site Generation (SSG)?
options: HTML pre-generated at build time and served as static files
quiz.answer: HTML pre-generated at build time and served as static files

quiz.q: What is a major SEO benefit of SSR over pure client-side rendering?
options: Search engine crawlers receive fully rendered HTML, improving indexability and SEO
quiz.answer: Search engine crawlers receive fully rendered HTML, improving indexability and SEO

quiz.q: In Next.js App Router, how do you create a '/about' page?
What is Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)?
options: A hybrid approach: pages are statically generated but revalidated at a defined interval without full rebuild
quiz.answer: A hybrid approach: pages are statically generated but revalidated at a defined interval without full rebuild

quiz.q: What is a 'Server Component' in Next.js App Router?
options: A component running on the client with useState
A component rendered on the server with direct data access, no client-side JavaScript sent for it
A component that fetches from a server
quiz.answer: A component rendered on the server with direct data access, no client-side JavaScript sent for it

quiz.q: How do you declare a 'Client Component' in Next.js App Router?
options: Add 'use client' at the top of the file
quiz.answer: Add 'use client' at the top of the file

quiz.q: What is the Next.js component better than a standard tag?
options: It provides automatic optimization: lazy loading, size optimization, format conversion (WebP), and preventing layout shift
quiz.answer: It provides automatic optimization: lazy loading, size optimization, format conversion (WebP), and preventing layout shift

quiz.q: What is the Next.js <Link> component used for?
options: Client-side navigation between pages without full page reloads
quiz.answer: Client-side navigation between pages without full page reloads

roadmap.title: Next.js API Routes & Full-Stack Features
roadmap.watch_focus: Build API endpoints inside Next.js (api/ folder), use middleware for authentication/redirects, implement authentication with NextAuth.js (Google OAuth or Credentials provider).
roadmap.practice_task: Add API routes to your Next.js blog: /api/posts (GET all, POST new), /api/posts/[id] (GET, PUT, DELETE). Add NextAuth for Google sign-in. Protect the post creation page with middleware that redirects unauthenticated users.

quiz.q: What are Next.js API Routes?
options: Built-in serverless functions at /api/* path that handle backend logic without a separate server
quiz.answer: Built-in serverless functions at /api/* path that handle backend logic without a separate server

quiz.q: What is Next.js Middleware?
options: Code that runs before a request is completed, used for auth checks, redirects, rewrites
quiz.answer: Code that runs before a request is completed, used for auth checks, redirects, rewrites

quiz.q: What is NextAuth.js?
options: A custom authentication system you build from scratch
A complete authentication library for Next.js supporting OAuth, credentials, and more
quiz.answer: A complete authentication library for Next.js supporting OAuth, credentials, and more

quiz.q: What is an OAuth provider in NextAuth.js?
options: An external auth provider (like Google, GitHub) that handles the authentication flow
quiz.answer: An external auth provider (like Google, GitHub) that handles the authentication flow

quiz.q: Where should you place an API route file in Next.js App Router?
What does 'getSession()' do in NextAuth?
options: Retrieves the current user's session data server-side
quiz.answer: Retrieves the current user's session data server-side

quiz.q: What HTTP methods can a Next.js API Route handle?
options: Any HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, etc.
quiz.answer: Any HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, etc.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of NEXTAUTH_SECRET in NextAuth configuration?
options: A random secret used to sign/encrypt session tokens – must be kept private
quiz.answer: A random secret used to sign/encrypt session tokens – must be kept private

quiz.q: What is a serverless function?
options: A function running on a server 24/7
A function that runs on-demand in the cloud without managing server infrastructure
A function with no server-side code
quiz.answer: A function that runs on-demand in the cloud without managing server infrastructure

quiz.q: How do you export a GET handler in a Next.js App Router API route file?

roadmap.title: Next.js Dynamic Routes, Layouts & Styles

playlist_videos.title: Styling in Next.js – CSS Modules, Tailwind

roadmap.watch_focus: Dynamic segments in Next.js ([id], [...slug]), catch-all routes, shared layouts with layout.js, nested layouts, styling options: CSS Modules, Tailwind CSS setup in Next.js.
roadmap.practice_task: Add dynamic routes to your blog (/blog/[slug]). Create a root layout with a persistent header/footer. Create a nested layout for the dashboard section. Add Tailwind CSS to the project and restyle the blog cards with Tailwind utility classes.

quiz.q: What is a dynamic route segment in Next.js?
options: A route that changes every hour
A file/folder name wrapped in square brackets (e.g., [id]) that matches any URL segment
quiz.answer: A file/folder name wrapped in square brackets (e.g., [id]) that matches any URL segment

quiz.q: What does a 'catch-all route' [...slug] match in Next.js?
options: Zero or more URL segments after the route prefix
quiz.answer: Zero or more URL segments after the route prefix

quiz.q: What is a 'layout.js' file in Next.js App Router?
options: A shared UI component that wraps page content and persists across route changes in that segment
quiz.answer: A shared UI component that wraps page content and persists across route changes in that segment

quiz.q: What is a CSS Module in Next.js?
options: A CSS file (.module.css) where class names are scoped locally to avoid conflicts
quiz.answer: A CSS file (.module.css) where class names are scoped locally to avoid conflicts

quiz.q: What is Tailwind CSS?
options: A CSS framework with pre-built components
A utility-first CSS framework where you apply small, single-purpose classes directly in HTML/JSX
quiz.answer: A utility-first CSS framework where you apply small, single-purpose classes directly in HTML/JSX

quiz.q: How do you access dynamic route params in a Next.js Server Component?
options: via 'params' prop: export default function Page({ params }) { return params.id }
quiz.answer: via 'params' prop: export default function Page({ params }) { return params.id }

quiz.q: What is the advantage of nested layouts in Next.js?
options: Different sections of your app can have their own persistent layout without re-rendering shared elements
quiz.answer: Different sections of your app can have their own persistent layout without re-rendering shared elements

quiz.q: What is the Tailwind class to add 'padding of 4 units' to all sides?
What file configures which paths Tailwind CSS scans for class names?
What is the difference between a Next.js 'page.js' and 'layout.js'?
options: page.js renders unique content for a route; layout.js wraps pages and persists across navigation in that route segment
quiz.answer: page.js renders unique content for a route; layout.js wraps pages and persists across navigation in that route segment

roadmap.title: Next.js Server Actions, Environment Variables & Deployment
roadmap.watch_focus: Server Actions for form submissions without API routes, environment variables (.env.local, NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix), deploying Next.js to Vercel with automatic CI/CD from GitHub.
roadmap.practice_task: Add a Server Action to your blog: when a logged-in user submits the 'create post' form, it runs a Server Action that saves to MongoDB directly (no API route needed). Set up all env vars in Vercel dashboard. Push to GitHub and watch auto-deploy.

quiz.q: What are Next.js Server Actions?
options: Async functions that run on the server, called directly from client components (often used for forms)
quiz.answer: Async functions that run on the server, called directly from client components (often used for forms)

quiz.q: How do you define a Server Action in a Next.js file?
options: Add 'use server' directive at the top of the file or function
quiz.answer: Add 'use server' directive at the top of the file or function

quiz.q: What prefix must you add to a Next.js environment variable to expose it to the BROWSER (client-side)?
Where should you store environment variables for local development in Next.js?
What is the main benefit of deploying Next.js on Vercel vs other platforms?
options: Vercel is built by the creators of Next.js, offering zero-config deployment, automatic CI/CD, and optimal Next.js performance
quiz.answer: Vercel is built by the creators of Next.js, offering zero-config deployment, automatic CI/CD, and optimal Next.js performance

quiz.q: What happens automatically when you push to your GitHub main branch on a Vercel-connected project?
options: A new production deployment is triggered automatically
quiz.answer: A new production deployment is triggered automatically

quiz.q: What is the benefit of Server Actions over traditional API routes for forms?
options: Server Actions reduce boilerplate: no need to write separate API endpoint, fetch call, and JSON parsing for simple form submissions
quiz.answer: Server Actions reduce boilerplate: no need to write separate API endpoint, fetch call, and JSON parsing for simple form submissions

quiz.q: What is 'revalidatePath' used for in Next.js Server Actions?
options: Revalidates cached data for a specific path after a mutation, so the page shows fresh data
quiz.answer: Revalidates cached data for a specific path after a mutation, so the page shows fresh data

quiz.q: What is the 'next.config.js' file used for?
options: Configuring Next.js behavior: redirects, rewrites, image domains, environment variables, webpack settings
quiz.answer: Configuring Next.js behavior: redirects, rewrites, image domains, environment variables, webpack settings

quiz.q: What does 'Preview Deployment' mean on Vercel?
options: Vercel automatically deploys every PR/branch to a unique URL for review before merging to production
A paid feature for previewing designs
quiz.answer: Vercel automatically deploys every PR/branch to a unique URL for review before merging to production

roadmap.title: Capstone Project – Build & Deploy a Full-Stack Next.js App

playlist_videos.title: Capstone Project: BitLinks – URL Shortener Part 1
Capstone Project: BitLinks – URL Shortener Part 2
Capstone Project: BitLinks – URL Shortener Part 3

roadmap.watch_focus: Build a complete production-grade Next.js full-stack app: URL shortener with authentication, MongoDB database, custom slug generation, redirect logic, link analytics, and full deployment.
roadmap.practice_task: Follow along and build the BitLinks URL Shortener. Ensure: Google Auth login, ability to create short links, redirect logic working, user dashboard showing their links, deployed live on Vercel with MongoDB Atlas.

quiz.q: In a URL shortener app, what is a 'slug'?
options: The short unique identifier appended to the base URL (e.g., bit.ly/abc123 → 'abc123' is the slug)
quiz.answer: The short unique identifier appended to the base URL (e.g., bit.ly/abc123 → 'abc123' is the slug)

quiz.q: What HTTP status code is used for a permanent redirect?
Which Next.js function performs a redirect from a server component or API route?
What npm package helps generate short unique IDs for URL slugs?
In the context of a URL shortener, what does 'analytics/click tracking' involve?
options: Incrementing a click count in the database each time the short URL is visited
quiz.answer: Incrementing a click count in the database each time the short URL is visited

quiz.q: What is the general system design of a URL shortener?
options: Store [shortSlug → originalURL] mapping in DB; on visit to /slug, look up DB and redirect to originalURL
quiz.answer: Store [shortSlug → originalURL] mapping in DB; on visit to /slug, look up DB and redirect to originalURL

quiz.q: What is the benefit of using MongoDB Atlas for the capstone project deployment?
options: It is a managed cloud database that works with deployed apps without managing infrastructure
It is faster than local MongoDB always
quiz.answer: It is a managed cloud database that works with deployed apps without managing infrastructure

quiz.q: What is 'middleware' useful for in the URL shortener Next.js project?
options: Intercepting requests to /[slug] routes, checking if the slug exists, and performing server-side redirects efficiently
quiz.answer: Intercepting requests to /[slug] routes, checking if the slug exists, and performing server-side redirects efficiently

quiz.q: Why should a 'User' field be stored with each short URL in the database?
options: To associate links with their creator so each user can see and manage only their own links
quiz.answer: To associate links with their creator so each user can see and manage only their own links

quiz.q: What should a production Next.js full-stack app always have before going live?
options: Environment variables configured in Vercel, MongoDB Atlas connection string, NextAuth secret, domain configured
quiz.answer: Environment variables configured in Vercel, MongoDB Atlas connection string, NextAuth secret, domain configured

roadmap.title: Capstone Project Continuation & Final Polish
roadmap.watch_focus: Build a Linktree-style profile page generator: user auth, dynamic public profile pages, adding/removing links, live preview, and full deployment.
roadmap.practice_task: Build the Linktree Clone fully. Your public profile page (/username) should be fully dynamic based on user data from MongoDB. Add social sharing meta tags (Open Graph) for link previews. Deploy and test the full flow end-to-end.

quiz.q: What is Open Graph (OG) metadata used for?
options: Rich link previews when shared on social media (title, description, image shown in chat apps)
quiz.answer: Rich link previews when shared on social media (title, description, image shown in chat apps)

quiz.q: How do you add Open Graph meta tags in a Next.js page?
options: Export a 'metadata' object from layout.js or page.js with openGraph properties
Use a third-party SEO plugin only
quiz.answer: Export a 'metadata' object from layout.js or page.js with openGraph properties

quiz.q: What does 'generateMetadata' function in Next.js allow?
options: Generating static metadata at build time only
Dynamically generating metadata per page (e.g., fetching page title from DB for a dynamic route)
quiz.answer: Dynamically generating metadata per page (e.g., fetching page title from DB for a dynamic route)

quiz.q: In a Linktree-style app, what makes the user's public profile URL dynamic?
options: A static HTML page per user
A dynamic route ([username]/page.js) that fetches the user's data from DB based on the URL slug
quiz.answer: A dynamic route ([username]/page.js) that fetches the user's data from DB based on the URL slug

quiz.q: What is the recommended way to handle 'not found' pages in Next.js App Router?
options: Create a 'not-found.js' file in the app directory, or call 'notFound()' in a server component
quiz.answer: Create a 'not-found.js' file in the app directory, or call 'notFound()' in a server component

quiz.q: What is optimistic UI in React?
options: Updating the UI immediately before server confirmation, then correcting if the request fails
quiz.answer: Updating the UI immediately before server confirmation, then correcting if the request fails

quiz.q: What should you test before declaring a full-stack app production-ready?
options: All CRUD flows, authentication, edge cases (empty states, invalid inputs), performance, and mobile responsiveness
quiz.answer: All CRUD flows, authentication, edge cases (empty states, invalid inputs), performance, and mobile responsiveness

quiz.q: What is a 'Skeleton Screen' in UI/UX?
options: A placeholder UI shown while content is loading, mimicking the shape of the final content
quiz.answer: A placeholder UI shown while content is loading, mimicking the shape of the final content

quiz.q: What does 'useOptimistic' hook (React 19/Next.js) do?
options: Provides a way to optimistically update UI while an async action is in progress
quiz.answer: Provides a way to optimistically update UI while an async action is in progress

quiz.q: What makes a web app 'full-stack'?
options: It includes both a frontend (client-side UI) and a backend (server, database, business logic) in one application
It is built with multiple programming languages
quiz.answer: It includes both a frontend (client-side UI) and a backend (server, database, business logic) in one application
